provenance.notes: First-pass identifier terminology reference. This file documents known identifier spaces and conversion status; it does not derive mappings.

evidence: Local source sub100 is exactly 0 through 99.
Local source day1 and week1 buckets are contiguous from 0.
Imported rescue bucket arrays in data/rescue-buckets.json match mooncat_rescueOrder_by_category.json exactly.
Imported character-cat arrays in data/character-cat-index.json match mooncat_rescueOrder_by_category.json exactly for checked categories.
Preferred API samples for 0, 82, 3364, 3365, 5683, 5684, 5754, 5755, and 25439 returned rescueOrder equal to the requested numeric index.
Preferred API year-boundary samples returned rescueYear values matching local year bucket boundaries.
Preferred API genesis samples at 84 and 2891 returned genesis: true with rescueOrder equal to the requested index.
limitations: This verifies numeric alignment for sampled API records and local bucket structure; it is not a full exhaustive API crawl.
This does not define conversion to bytes5 cat IDs, ERC-721 token IDs, OpenSea IDs, wrapper IDs, or contract call values.

evidence: Etherscan page for 0x60cd862c9C687A9dE49aecdC3A99b74A4fc54aB6 identifies a verified contract address with public name tag MoonCatRescue: MoonCatRescue.
Etherscan page for 0xc3f733ca98E0daD0386979Eb96fb1722A1A05E69 identifies Acclimated Mooncats as a verified token contract.
LooksRare collection URL uses 0xc3f733ca98E0daD0386979Eb96fb1722A1A05E69 for MoonCats.
OpenSea item URLs sampled for token IDs 0, 82, and 25439 use the acclimated contract address and the numeric token ID in the URL.
Acclimated MoonCatAcclimator source says wrap(_rescueOrder) returns the ID/rescue order of the minted token.
Acclimated MoonCatAcclimator source mints _tokenId in _wrap(address _owner, uint256 _tokenId), where _tokenId is documented as the rescue order of the MoonCat.

Preferred API /mooncat/erc721-metadata/{index} samples for 0, 82, and 25439 returned ERC721-style metadata whose name, external_url, image URL, and Rescue Index attribute use the requested numeric rescue index.
limitations: OpenSea token ID behavior is verified for sampled acclimated MoonCat item URLs, not for unrelated collections or historical wrappers.
This does not define conversion from rescue order/token ID to bytes5 catId.
This does not verify the older wrapper contract token ID convention beyond the source comment that an older wrapper existed at 0x7C40c3...

identifierKinds.label: bytes5 MoonCat ID
identifierKinds.notes: The contract source uses bytes5 cat IDs. The API landing pages document a valid MoonCat ID example for catId_or_rescueIndex. This file does not define the full catId parser or any reverse mapping.

identifierKinds.label: API original rescue index
identifierKinds.notes: The API landing pages document rescueIndex values from 0 through 25439 for catId_or_rescueIndex. Local bucket checks and preferred API samples support alignment between this API rescueOrder convention and local rescue-order-index values.

identifierKinds.label: local rescue-order index
identifierKinds.notes: Local membership and rescue bucket files use indexKind rescue-order-index. Local bucket checks and preferred API samples support alignment with the API rescueOrder/original rescue index convention. These values are still not token IDs, OpenSea IDs, bytes5 catIds, or contract call values.

identifierKinds.label: Acclimated ERC-721 token ID
usedBy: marketplace item URLs for the acclimated collection
identifierKinds.notes: For the current Acclimated MoonCats contract, Etherscan source shows the ERC-721 tokenId is the MoonCat rescue order. This does not apply automatically to older wrappers or unrelated token-facing surfaces.

identifierKinds.label: OpenSea token ID
usedBy: OpenSea item URLs for the acclimated MoonCats collection
identifierKinds.notes: Sampled OpenSea item URLs use /item/ethereum/0xc3f733ca98e0dad0386979eb96fb1722a1a05e69/{tokenId}; token IDs 0, 82, and 25439 resolved to MoonCat pages with matching numeric names/metadata. Treat this as sampled evidence for the acclimated collection, not a blanket marketplace rule.

identifierKinds.label: Original MoonCatRescue contract address
identifierKinds.notes: Verified as the Ethereum mainnet original MoonCatRescue contract address from the registered Etherscan source. This is a contract address, not a MoonCat identifier.

identifierKinds.label: Acclimated MoonCats contract address
identifierKinds.notes: Verified from the registered Etherscan source and marketplace URLs. This is a contract address, not a token ID.

identifierKinds.label: Ethereum address
identifierKinds.notes: Used for owner/profile and owned-MoonCat API lookups. This file does not document checksum, lowercase, or ENS normalization behavior.

identifierKinds.label: accessory ID
identifierKinds.notes: API paths document accessoryId parameters. This pass does not define accessory ID format, range, contract origin, or validation rules.

knownParameters.notes: API landing pages document this parameter as accepting either a valid MoonCat ID or original rescue index.

conversionRules.notes: Local bucket structure and preferred API samples support numeric alignment between API rescueOrder/original rescue index and local rescue-order-index. This does not imply conversion to bytes5 catIds or token-facing IDs.

Local rescue-order-index values can be treated as API rescueOrder/original rescue index values for the checked convention. This does not make them token IDs, OpenSea IDs, bytes5 catIds, or contract call values.
A verified source query or parser-derived mapping is required before using local indexes as cat IDs.
For the current Acclimated MoonCats ERC-721 contract, tokenId is the rescue order/local rescue-order index. This does not apply automatically to older wrappers or unrelated contracts.
Sampled OpenSea acclimated collection URLs use the acclimated contract address plus numeric token ID matching rescue order. This is sampled marketplace evidence, not a general OpenSea rule.

relatedFiles.relationship: documents API parameters and endpoint usage
uses local rescue-order-index rescue/history bucket arrays
documents bytes5 cat ID generation facts from contract source

todos: Document the exact derivation method for local rescue-order-index arrays.
Verify older wrapper contract token ID conventions separately.
Document any source-backed conversion between bytes5 catIds and index-based identifiers.
